narrative: OMX30 faces a classic transatlantic conflict: European bourses ended flat-to-positive yesterday, but US equities rolled over hard (-1-2%) on trade war fears and rising yields (10Y +99bp to 4.703%). The index sits +6.5% above its 200-MA in bull market structure, yet technical deterioration is evident—MACD below signal, ADX 37.7 with bearish DI dominance, and RSI 41 suggesting neutral momentum with upside exhaustion. Brent's 7.96% collapse is a double-edged sword: positive for exporter margins but signaling demand destruction. With VIX up 102bp and tariff headlines bleeding into Friday's open, expect a cautious tone; the index is only 1.9% from 52-week highs and vulnerable to mean reversion.
morningBrief: OMX30 opens in a risk-off crosswind. US equity bloodbath (NASDAQ -215bp, S&P -121bp) on Trump tariff escalation and rising real yields will weigh on opening, despite relative strength in DAX (+53bp) and FTSE (+30bp). ADX at 37.7 shows directional conviction but the bearish DI bias is a red flag—expect sellers above 3150. Key support at 3085 (lower Bollinger) and 3123 (yesterday's close) are critical holds. Industrials (Volvo, Atlas Copco) face headwinds from tariff uncertainty despite USD/SEK strength at 9.734. Banks may see NIM support from 4.7% yields, but real estate is a clear underperform. Copper +69bp keeps Boliden on watch, but oil's 8% collapse pressures sentiment. Watch US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET—a miss could accelerate risk-off. Trade cautiously; conviction is low with earnings season winding down and macro noise dominating.
drivers: US equity selloff (-1.21% S&P, -2.15% NASDAQ) creating risk-off headwind
Negative correlation signal (-0.639%) despite European resilience
Strong ADX (37.7) with bearish directional bias offsetting bull market structure
Oil collapse (-7.96% Brent) supporting exporters but pressuring consumer spending
US tariff escalation (60 countries) creating macro uncertainty for Swedish industrials

sectorImpacts.reason: US 10Y Yield at 4.703% (+99bp) expands NIM outlook despite risk-off sentiment. Swedish deposit spreads remain attractive.
Trade war escalation (60 countries hit with tariffs) creates margin uncertainty. USD/SEK strength (+39bp) offers partial offset but insufficient to overcome macro headwinds.
Rising yields (10Y +99bp) increase refinancing costs and compress property valuations. Risk-off sentiment adds pressure.
NASDAQ -215bp overnight creates negative sentiment spillover. Hexagon particularly exposed to tech multiples compression.
Copper +69bp and gold +8bp provide direct revenue lift for Boliden. SSAB benefits from oil weakness (input cost relief), partially offset by demand concerns.
Oil collapse (-7.96%) reduces spending pressure, but risk-off sentiment and tariff uncertainty weigh on discretionary demand. Weak EUR/SEK (+20bp) adds sourcing headwind for H&M.
Defensive rotation bid expected in risk-off environment. Flight-to-safety should support large-cap pharma despite negative macro backdrop.

watchList.reason: Copper +69bp and gold +8bp deliver direct commodity tailwinds. Watch for any tariff-related demand destruction in copper markets.
watchList.catalyst: Commodities strength, tariff impact on industrial demand

watchList.reason: NIM expansion story intact at 4.703% yields. Selective accumulation on weakness if risk-off deepens; strong liquidity franchise.
Tariff escalation headline creates near-term uncertainty. USD/SEK strength (+39bp) offers partial support but insufficient. Watch for guidance revisions.
watchList.catalyst: Trade war escalation, automotive tariff exposure

watchList.reason: Real estate underperform on rising yields. 4.7% cost of capital pressure is structural headwind; avoid accumulation until yields stabilize.
Defensive positioning in risk-off environment. Large-cap pharma with global revenue streams should outperform cyclicals.
Mixed signals: oil weakness reduces consumer pressure, but weak EUR/SEK (+20bp) and tariff uncertainty create sourcing headwinds. Range-bound volatility expected.
watchList.catalyst: FX dynamics, tariff impact on imports

keyLevels.resistance: 3150 (intraday resistance, ADX bearish bias suggests sellers)

econCalendarAlert: US Jobless Claims (14:30 CET) is the key macro event. Miss would accelerate risk-off; beat could stabilize sentiment. No major Swedish releases.
topRisk: Trade war escalation spreading to additional sectors or markets (e.g., retaliatory tariffs on EU). A breach below 3085 would trigger capitulation selling and test 200-MA support at ~3000.
technicalSummary: Bull market structure intact: +6.5% above 200-MA, +1.3% above 50-MA
Deteriorating momentum: MACD below signal (hist -1.941), RSI 41 (neutral, no mean-reversion trigger)
ADX 37.7 strong but bearish DI bias signals directional weakness despite elevated volatility
%B 0.57 in upper half of bands; Stochastic neutral (K 55.6, D 47.2) offers no conviction
ROC-90 +5.5% shows 3-month momentum intact, but price near 52-week highs (92% of range) is exhaustion signal
Correlation signal -0.639% confirms risk-off, despite Europe's relative outperformance vs US
